candidates.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue
candidates.why: This is clearly a component of non-interest income, not total revenue. The true top-line such as 'Total income' is not among the candidates, so no candidate correctly represents total revenue/sales.
A balancing item is an adjustment, not an economic revenue line. Using this as REVT would misrepresent top-line. The correct revenue line exists in the statement but is not in the candidate list.

variables.notes: The proper top-line revenue/total income line is present in the income statement (e.g., 'Total income') but is not included in the REVT candidate list. Neither of the provided candidates appropriately represents total revenue, so REVT is left unmapped and flagged for manual review.
This is a bank; no conventional COGS line is present and no candidates were provided. Treat COGS as zero.

candidates.why: This is income, not an operating expense. SG&A should capture operating overhead expenses, so this should be excluded.
This is also income, not overhead expense. Should not be used for SG&A components.
This is clearly labeled as income, not expense. Exclude from SG&A.
A classic SG&A component: personnel-related overhead excluding direct COGS. Appropriate to include as an SG&A component.
Generic operating expense bucket likely capturing overhead. Appropriate SG&A component (not labeled as total).
Administrative/overhead costs are SG&A by definition. Good SG&A component.
Explicit operating expense bucket, likely overhead. Appropriate for SG&A, distinct from COGS in a bank context.
Residual operating expense bucket that functions as SG&A-type overhead. Suitable as an SG&A component.

This appears to be a generic bucket and may overlap with the detailed components and with 'Total operating expenses'. To avoid double-counting, prefer the more granular component rows instead of this subtotal-like label.
Likely small additional administrative overhead costs, fitting SG&A. Appropriate to include as a component.
Share-based compensation is typically classified within SG&A. Include as a distinct SG&A component if populated in any year.

variables.notes: Selected only expense/overhead lines and avoided income and explicit totals. Excluded 'Operating Expenses' to reduce risk of double-counting given presence of detailed component rows and a separate 'Total operating expenses' line.
No research & development line is present in the statements and no candidates were provided. Treat XRD as zero.

candidates.why: This is a specific interest income component, not the aggregate interest expense. Prefer the main interest expense line for XINT.
This is interest and fee income on loans (an asset-side income). Not suitable as interest expense.
This is the primary aggregate interest expense line on the income statement, matching the XINT definition best.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses on liabilities to credit institutions
candidates.why: Component of interest expense, but XINT should preferably use the total interest expense line instead of a component to capture all interest costs.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses related to the issuance of securities
candidates.why: Another component of interest expense, but the aggregate 'Interest expenses' line is more appropriate for XINT.
Specific subset of interest expense. Prefer the total interest expense line for XINT.
Net interest is interest income minus interest expense. XINT should be gross interest expense, not net.
This is a tax item related to hybrid capital, not interest expense.
Represents profit attribution, not interest expense.
Same as above, profit attribution, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: This is indeed interest expense but only for lease liabilities. Since a total interest expense line is available, that total is preferred for XINT.
Label unclear, but likely refers to some interest-related amount; however, the clearly defined 'Interest expenses' line is preferable.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: Balance sheet equity investments, not interest expense.
A balance sheet accrual, not the P&L interest expense flow.
Equity attributable to minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as above; equity, not interest.

variables.notes: Mapped XINT to the aggregate 'Interest expenses' line to capture total interest cost rather than individual components or net interest.

candidates.row_label: Attributable to Equity capital certificate holders
candidates.why: This is an income attribution line (part of comprehensive income), not a stock of equity on the balance sheet.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: This is an asset (investments in equity securities), not the firm’s own equity.
This is a component of equity (similar to share capital) but not total book equity. Prefer a total equity line for BE.
Another component of equity. Better to use the total equity line for BE.

Appears to represent parent equity excluding non-controlling interests (31,165). However, a separate 'Total equity' (31,865) includes non-controlling interests, which is closer to total book equity of the firm.

This is the total equity including non-controlling interests (31,865), which best matches the definition of book equity for the firm as a whole. MIB is captured separately, so BE can be based on this total and adjustments handled in modeling.

variables.notes: Selected the broader 'Total equity' line (including non-controlling interests). Minority interest is separately mapped to MIB, so any needed adjustment can be made downstream.

candidates.why: This is the income attributable to minority interests, not the balance sheet stock of minority equity. For MIB, prefer the balance sheet minority equity line.
Same as above: income statement attribution, not the equity amount. Use balance sheet line instead.

candidates.row_label: Shares, units and other equity interests
candidates.why: Represents equity investments on the asset side, not minority interest in the firm’s equity.
This is the balance sheet equity attributable to non-controlling (minority) shareholders, which matches the MIB definition.
Alternative label for the same concept as 'Non-controlling interests'. Either would be correct, but we choose one to avoid duplication.

variables.notes: Mapped MIB to the balance sheet 'Non-controlling interests' line, which directly represents minority equity.
